title: QPortfolio Scenario Data
description: Root document for a QPortfolio scenario file (qp_file_type = "QPortfolio Scenario Data"). A scenario is one "what-if" case within a portfolio: its settings, optimization configuration, metric limits, the selected opportunities, and any selection rules overridden from the portfolio defaults. All opportunity and metric names must match those defined in the portfolio.

metadata.description: Document header. For a scenario file qp_file_type must be "QPortfolio Scenario Data".

qp_file_type.description: Discriminator for the document kind. Use "QPortfolio Data" for a portfolio-definition file (PortfolioData) and "QPortfolio Scenario Data" for a scenario file (ScenarioData). Required.

qp_version.description: QPortfolio schema/format version this file targets, e.g. 4.5. Optional; when omitted the importer assumes the current version.

settings.description: Scenario identity and case settings (name, colour, data version, characteristics).

scenario_name.description: Unique, human-readable name of the scenario.

color.description: Display colour for the scenario as a hex string, e.g. "#C25A38". Optional.

description.description: Free-text description of the scenario. Optional.

instance_selection_threshold.description: Opportunity Instance Selection Threshold: the lowest fractional interest (between 0 and 1) counted as one instance when the opportunity is selected. Interests below it count as zero instances. Must be greater than the optimization feasibility_tolerance. Optional.

enable_soft_constraints.description: When true, soft metric limits are allowed to be violated for a penalty in this scenario. Optional; defaults to false.

data_version_name.description: Name of the data version this scenario loads for calculation/optimization (the input/master data set used).

characteristics.description: Scenario-level attribute/characteristic selections (e.g. a price set), driving sensitivity without changing opportunity inputs. Optional.

items.description: A single attribute/value pair, e.g. attribute = "Country", value = "Canada".

value.description: The characteristic value for this attribute. Null/absent means unset.

optimization.description: Optimization configuration: the objective metric and direction plus solver settings.

direction.description: Whether to maximize or minimize the objective metric. Defaults to maximize. Allowed values: `Maximize` — Maximize the objective metric (e.g. maximize NPV).; `Minimize` — Minimize the objective metric (e.g. minimize spend)..

objective_metric_name.description: Name of the metric to optimize (the objective function), e.g. an NPV metric.

objective_time_period.description: For a time-series objective, the period (time-step) up to which the metric is optimized. Optional for scalar objective metric.

feasibility_tolerance.description: Feasibility tolerance: how strictly constraints must be honoured. Larger values can make a previously infeasible model solvable and solve faster. Default ~1E-7. Must be smaller than the instance_selection_threshold. Optional.

optimality_tolerance.description: Optimality tolerance: tolerance on the nonlinear solver's rate-of-improvement test. Smaller values usually run longer but can yield better solutions. Default ~1E-4. Optional.

optimality_gap_absolute.description: Absolute optimality gap: stop once the gap between the objective value and the best bound is within this absolute amount. Optional.

optimality_gap_percentage.description: Relative optimality gap (percent): stop once the objective value is within this percentage of the best bound. Optional.

penalty_weight.description: Overall scenario soft-constraints penalty weight applied across all soft metric limits. Optional.

progress_interval.description: Solver progress check interval: how often optimization status is updated. Smaller values tend to increase solution time. Optional.

timeout_minutes.description: Maximum minutes the solver may search. On timeout, an integer model returns the best integer solution found so far. Default 1. Optional.

enforce_timeout.description: When true, forcibly stop all solver sub-problems at once on timeout (risking loss of feasible solutions) rather than letting each honour its own timeout. Optional; defaults to false.

load_selections.description: When true, load the scenario's current selections as the solver's starting point to improve performance. Optional; defaults to false.

use_linearization.description: When true, replace nonlinear operations with equivalent linear ones for performance (see linearization_level). Optional; defaults to false.

linearization_level.description: Which functions to linearize when use_linearization is true. Optional. Allowed values: `SolverDecide` — Let the solver decide which functions to linearize.; `AbsMinMax` — Linearize the ABS, MAX and MIN functions only.; `All` — Linearize all supported functions..

use_local_solver.description: When true, use local solver(s) for a faster near-global result instead of the default global solver. Optional; defaults to false.

max_local_solver.description: Number of local solvers to run (up to 100) when use_local_solver is true. Optional.

quadratic_xor_dependency.description: When true, model 'must not' selection dependencies with a quadratic expression (a*b = 0) instead of a Lindo Special Ordered Set; one form may solve faster depending on the model. Optional; defaults to false.

strict_selection_threshold.description: When true, strictly enforce the instance_selection_threshold by overriding the feasibility tolerance. Can be performance-sensitive. Optional; defaults to false.

solver_parameters.description: Expert Lindo solver parameters (id/value pairs). For advanced tuning; refer to Lindo documentation. Optional.

items.description: An expert Lindo solver parameter as an id/value pair.

metric_limits.description: Metric limits/targets the optimizer must honour for this scenario (per-metric, per-period).

items.description: A limit or target placed on a metric's value (per period) that the optimizer must honour. A limit may be made soft to allow violation for a penalty.

disabled.description: When true the limit is present but ignored by the optimizer. Optional; defaults to false.

metric_name.description: Name of the metric being limited. Must match a model metric name.

limit_type.description: Whether targets are a minimum (lower bound) or maximum (upper bound). Allowed values: `Minimum` — The metric value must be at least the target (lower bound).; `Maximum` — The metric value must be at most the target (upper bound)..

unit.description: Unit label for the targets, e.g. "$ MM". Optional.

targets.description: The per-period target values. Periods not listed are unconstrained for this metric.

items.description: A single per-period target value for a metric limit.

period.description: Time period this target applies to (0-based).

value.description: The target value the metric is bounded to in this period (interpreted as a minimum or maximum per the limit's limit_type).

disabled.description: When true this period's target is present but ignored. Optional; defaults to false.

soft.description: When true this limit may be violated for a penalty (a soft constraint) rather than being strictly enforced. Requires the scenario's enable_soft_constraints. Optional; defaults to false.

magnitude.description: For a soft limit, the magnitude (scale of allowed violation) used in the penalty calculation. Optional; applies only when soft is true.

penalty_weight.description: For a soft limit, the penalty weight applied to violations of this limit against the objective; higher weights make the limit 'harder'. Optional; applies only when soft is true.

opportunity_selections.description: The selected quantity of each opportunity by period — the optimization result or a manually edited set of selections.

items.description: The selected quantity of one opportunity across periods.

opportunity_name.description: Name of the selected opportunity. Must match a defined opportunity.

selections.description: The per-period selection values. An empty list means the opportunity is not selected in any period.

items.description: The selected quantity of an opportunity in a single period.

value.description: Selected quantity (interest) for this period. 1 = a full selection; a fraction (e.g. 0.5) = a partial selection where allowed; 0 = not selected.

selection_constraints.description: Selection constraints overridden for this scenario if present(not null) (otherwise inherited from the portfolio).

items.description: Selection limits scoped to one named opportunity.

opportunity_name.description: Unique name of the opportunity these limits apply to.

disabled.description: When true the constraint is present but ignored. Optional; defaults to false.

integer_only.description: When true the optimizer may only make whole (integer) selections of this unit — no partial/fractional selection. Integer selections increase solve time.

interior_limit.description: Min Fraction: the minimum quantity that must be selected IF the unit is selected (it does not force selection). Applies per instance and only when non-integer selection is allowed; ignored when integer_only is true. Optional.

total_minimum.description: Minimum total selection summed across the entire span of eligible periods (aggregate, not per period). A positive value forces selection.

total_maximum.description: Maximum total selection summed across the entire span of eligible periods (aggregate, not per period). 0 forbids selection. Must be >= total_minimum.

total_instances_minimum.description: Minimum number of instances (each in-period selection counts as one instance regardless of fractional size; 1.1 counts as two). A positive value forces selection. Optional.

total_instances_maximum.description: Maximum number of instances allowed. Optional. Must be >= total_instances_minimum.

time_period_minima.description: Per-period minimum selections, indexed by time period (element 0 = period 0). A positive entry forces selection in that period. May be empty (no per-period minima) or shorter than the horizon (unspecified periods are unbounded below).

time_period_maxima.description: Per-period maximum selections, indexed by time period (element 0 = period 0). Caps selection in that period (0 forbids it). May be empty (no per-period maxima). Where present, each entry must be >= the matching time_period_minima.

selection_dependency.description: Selection dependencies overridden for this scenario if present(not null) (otherwise inherited from the portfolio).

items.description: One selection-dependency rule. Read as: for the dependent opportunity to be selected, the independent opportunity must/must-not be selected, starting timing_offset periods before/after/during the dependent's selected period, in the given quantity ratio.

disabled.description: When true the rule is present but ignored. Optional; defaults to false.

dependent_opportunity.description: Name of the dependent opportunity — the one whose selection triggers the rule ("to select X…"). X is the dependent.

independent_opportunity.description: Name of the independent opportunity the dependent's selection depends on.

type.description: Whether the independent opportunity must, or must not, be selected. Allowed values: `Must` — The independent opportunity MUST be selected for the dependent to be selected.; `MustNot` — The independent opportunity MUST NOT be selected when the dependent is selected (an exclusive either/or condition)..

timing.description: Range direction of the timing relationship (over-all / before / after / during). Allowed values: `OverAll` — The relationship applies across all periods (no directional timing).; `Before` — The independent selection must occur before (inclusively) the dependent's period.; `After` — The independent selection must occur after (inclusively) the dependent's period.; `During` — The independent selection must occur during (the same period as) the dependent's period..

timing_offset.description: Signed number of periods locating the start of the constrained range relative to the dependent's selected period: -1 = starts one period prior, 1 = starts one period after, 0 = same period. The sign only locates the start; it does not encode direction (that is the timing field).

each_scale.description: Ratio scale on the dependent side ('each' clause). With each_interest, expresses the dependent quantity in the 'N instances / R selections' ratio. Optional.

each_interest.description: When true the 'each' ratio is expressed in real-number selections (R, interest); when false it is expressed in instance counts (N).

need_scale.description: Ratio scale on the independent side ('need' clause), paired with need_interest.

need_interest.description: When true the 'need' ratio is expressed in real-number selections (R, interest); when false it is expressed in instance counts (N).

selection_group.description: Selection groups overridden for this scenario if present(not null) (otherwise inherited from the portfolio).

groups.description: The selection groups and their members.

items.description: One selection group: a named set of member opportunities linked by the group type.

disabled.description: When true the group is present but its linking behaviour is ignored (members fall back to their individual constraints). Optional; defaults to false.

group_name.description: Unique name of the group. Referenced by group_limits.group_name.

type.description: Whether membership is mutually inclusive or mutually exclusive. Allowed values: `MutualInclusive` — Mutually inclusive: all members are selected if any member is selected, in the same period as the independent opportunity.; `MutualExclusive` — Mutually exclusive: only one member may be selected; the others are excluded and remain unavailable in later periods..

members.description: The member opportunities of this group.

items.description: One member of a selection group.

opportunity_name.description: Name of the member opportunity. Must match a defined opportunity.

disabled.description: When true this membership is removed from the group and the opportunity's own selection constraints re-apply. Optional; defaults to false.

group_limits.description: Group-level selection limits, one per group, keyed by group_name. While a group's limits are active they take precedence over members' individual selection constraints.

items.description: Group-level selection limits applied to all members of one group. Inherits the same min/max/instance/per-period bounds as an opportunity limit; a group Max applies to each member (e.g. Max = 2 means each selected member may be selected up to twice), not to the count of members chosen.

group_name.description: Name of the group these limits apply to. Must match a group's group_name.

disabled.description: When true the constraint is present but ignored. Optional; defaults to false.

integer_only.description: When true the optimizer may only make whole (integer) selections of this unit — no partial/fractional selection. Integer selections increase solve time.

interior_limit.description: Min Fraction: the minimum quantity that must be selected IF the unit is selected (it does not force selection). Applies per instance and only when non-integer selection is allowed; ignored when integer_only is true. Optional.

total_minimum.description: Minimum total selection summed across the entire span of eligible periods (aggregate, not per period). A positive value forces selection.

total_maximum.description: Maximum total selection summed across the entire span of eligible periods (aggregate, not per period). 0 forbids selection. Must be >= total_minimum.

total_instances_minimum.description: Minimum number of instances (each in-period selection counts as one instance regardless of fractional size; 1.1 counts as two). A positive value forces selection. Optional.

total_instances_maximum.description: Maximum number of instances allowed. Optional. Must be >= total_instances_minimum.

time_period_minima.description: Per-period minimum selections, indexed by time period (element 0 = period 0). A positive entry forces selection in that period. May be empty (no per-period minima) or shorter than the horizon (unspecified periods are unbounded below).

time_period_maxima.description: Per-period maximum selections, indexed by time period (element 0 = period 0). Caps selection in that period (0 forbids it). May be empty (no per-period maxima). Where present, each entry must be >= the matching time_period_minima.
